coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 8–10 miles, Z2 (120–135 bpm), mostly flat or rolling terrain. You ran 20 miles with 2,989 ft vert yesterday at 136 bpm average—a solid near-race-pace effort for week 24—so today is structural recovery, not a throwaway. Your resting HR is 59 bpm (solid) but sleep was only 6.07 hours and recovery score sits at 63 (moderate), which means your CNS is not fully primed. Skip any tempo or threshold work; the taper phase prioritizes retention of fitness with minimal accumulated fatigue. Stick to conversational pace, focus on leg turnover and ease, and call it done by mile 10. This week's volume should drop 30–40% from last week's 57.1 miles as you wind toward 74 days out.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Post-run today: 40–50g carbs + 20–25g protein within 30 minutes (e.g., rice cakes with almond butter, banana with Greek yogurt). Because you logged a big glycogen spend yesterday and sleep depth was below your 7-hour average, prioritize carb replenishment at lunch and dinner—aim for 5–7g carbs per kg body weight across the day to fully restock. During today's easy 8–10 miler, water only if you're out >75 minutes; no need to fuel an easy run. Stay hydrated across meals; your resting HR improvement (59 vs. your 30-day avg of 61.2) suggests decent autonomic recovery, but short sleep can mask true fatigue state.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Tonight is non-negotiable: target 7.5–8 hours in bed. Your 6.07-hour night was efficient (92.5%), but sleep volume is the limiter in taper—quality matters less than quantity when you're 74 days out. No caffeine after 2 p.m., dim lights by 10 p.m., and consider a 20-minute afternoon nap if work travel permits. Your moderate recovery score (63) likely reflects the 20-miler's muscular load on top of travel-induced sleep fragmentation; sleep debt compounds in taper and blunts your ability to hold sharpness. A good night tonight sets up Tuesday's quality work correctly.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in taper now—the mind game shifts from "am I fit enough?" to "am I staying sharp?" Yesterday's 20 miler at 136 bpm average, with 2,989 ft vert, proved you have the engine for the Julian Alps' 2,500m demand. Today's easy run isn't a confidence killer; it's a refresh. Use it to dial in your pre-dawn routine, test your gear, and visualize a section of the course. The anxiety spike in taper is real and normal; your recovery score at 63 and short sleep will amplify it. Trust the plan—74 days of taper is where discipline meets restraint. Don't run extra tomorrow to prove you're ready.
